prompt: After eradication tasks complete, analysts must confirm no re-infection before closure. What validation practice should gate incident closure?
choices: Post-containment monitoring, control verification, and documented closure criteria review
Immediate closure once the incident commander updates the priority field manually
Archiving all child tasks before root cause analysis or lessons learned begin
Skipping timeline validation to accelerate quarterly executive metric reporting
explanation: Closure validation confirms eradication effectiveness and monitors for recurrence before security incidents are marked complete.

prompt: A municipal agency plans its first SIR deployment across three SOC shifts. How should the implementer sequence rollout?
choices: Pilot with one shift and core integrations, then expand playbooks, training, and coverage
Enable every automation and integration in production on day one without validation
Deploy only executive dashboards before any analyst workspace or process configuration
Skip sub-production testing because store integrations are always read-only
explanation: Phased SIR rollout validates workflows with a pilot group before expanding integrations, automation, and analyst adoption across shifts.

prompt: Architecture review maps SIEM alerts, SOAR playbooks, and threat feeds into SIR. What planning artifact should document that integration design?
choices: An integration roadmap covering data flows, ownership, environments, and cutover milestones
A list of dashboard portlet colors without inbound or outbound action definitions
Discovery schedule intervals for printer CI identification on office subnets only
Service catalog item descriptions for employee mobile device enrollment requests
explanation: Integration planning documents alert sources, orchestration touchpoints, and phased deployment so SIEM, SOAR, and TI align with SIR workflows.

prompt: Analysts resist new SIR workspace workflows after a major incident strained the SOC. What change management approach improves adoption?
choices: Role-based training, runbook walkthroughs, and feedback loops tied to live response drills
Mandate immediate closure of all legacy spreadsheets without any hands-on practice
Disable playbooks until every analyst completes unrelated HR compliance courses
Remove workspace access for senior analysts so junior staff learn through trial alone
explanation: SIR adoption improves when teams receive targeted training, practice with runbooks, and refine workflows based on operational feedback.

prompt: The CISO wants monthly proof that SIR investment is working. Which KPI set should the program report?
choices: Mean time to detect, mean time to contain, reopen rate, and severity trend analysis
Total analyst comment count per incident without severity or category segmentation
Number of dashboard portlets on the executive homepage without workflow context
Average age of resolved HR onboarding cases unrelated to security operations
explanation: SIR program KPIs track detection speed, containment effectiveness, closure quality, and severity patterns to demonstrate operational maturity.
